title: armeria maritima subsp. langei
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. langei, also known as Sea Thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Plumbaginaceae family. It is a perennial, low-growing herb with pink or white flowers that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Sea Thrift include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wound healing, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of Sea Thrift include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used to promote overall health and well-being, and are believed to have benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
